content:
Hi! Recently I wrote some super basic bindings for DRM's fourcc headers (basically, headers around framebuffer format and modifier codes). I asked this in a different conversation, but that was more around trying to figure out the capabilities of bindgen and it seems like the result of that discussion was mainly that bindgen is probably not capable of this.
Basically: there's quite a number of different format codes within https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/master/include/uapi/drm/drm_fourcc.h and I'd like to write something something in rust to read this header and autogenerate constants and possibly enum values for all of the codes in here. Since bindgen doesn't seem capable of handling this as a result of getting confused by the fourcc_code function - I was wondering if we have any way in the kernel of using rust for codegen that I might be able to take advantage of? Writing a text parser for this seems like it would be very easy

For userspace there is the drm-fourcc crate. Maybe you could do somethibg similar to what it does: Use regexes to extract all names, generate a C file which uses them all, run the preprocessor on it and finally use regexes to extract the right values (or use bindgen for the last step) https://github.com/dzfranklin/drm-fourcc-rs/blob/main/build.rs

bjorn3 said:

For userspace there is the drm-fourcc crate. Maybe you could do somethibg similar to what it does: Use regexes to extract all names, generate a C file which uses them all, run the preprocessor on it and finally use regexes to extract the right values (or use bindgen for the last step) https://github.com/dzfranklin/drm-fourcc-rs/blob/main/build.rs

Oh yeah I mean that was my general plan (though I'm probably just using rust's core string parsing library, tbh I don't often find myself needing to resort to regex in rust :) but my bigger question was around like, how would one do this from Kbuild and whether we have any prior examples of that?
scripts/generate_rust_target.rs is somewhat similar. rust host program which generates fiels for the build
That sounds like something that cmacro could do and it's author is working on integrating this crate into bindgen
if that's the case, then we would have an extra argument to move forward with that integration.

no, that would only let you evaluate "simple" function-like define directives like:

# define foo(x) bar(x)

the fourcc_code funcition also does bitwise shifts and castings so it wouldn't be enough, unless I forgot what the PR actually does :sweat_smile:
it actually solves it but maybe in an unsatisfactory way
as you still have to figure out how to link the whole thing in such a way that the wrappers are actually inlined. So that's either LTO or @Gary Guo's hack
cmacro would translate it directly to rust code
almost like a very lite version of c2rust
